channel.title: Comments for page "Where They Kept Their Copies"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Where They Kept Their Copies"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I feel bad for praising that above anything else. :C Your writing was quite worthy of an upvote all by itself!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shoo! Get out of here, ya Gamer!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang it Armstrong, you increased the duration of my Youtube videos by 5 seconds.

DARN YOU.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt a bit the same - initially excited about the prospect of new excerpts, and then a bit disappointed that the ones we got were familiar. However as someone who has considered trying to replicate that sweet, clean Star Signals smell, I can confirm that it's very difficult to match Silber's original without sounding obvious about it.

That said, what swung my upvote was the wind of bees later in the tale - that had the right feel about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you know that world-renowned writer Stephen King was once hit by a car? Just something to consider.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this enough to take the effort to log in and give a +1. Honestly, I was almost messaged Silberescher and try bribing him to write the book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Credit for those goes to Silber, as they are from the Star Signals SCP article.

Glad you feel they spice the tale up rather than bogging it down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna echo Roget here, liked it as a draft, like it even more now. I really enjoy the way that this wraps up, and as I said previously, the part about the little girl and her father still remains my favorite part about this tale. Everything comes together here nicely, and that last line is pretty great. +1 wholeheartedly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the excerpts that really make this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "And YOU get a Star Signals! And YOU get a Star Signals! EVERYBODY gets a Star Signals!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamn I love me some Fifth Church bullshit. I especially like the father and his daughter running through the field, and her seriously frightened by what he's doing and going through. I think the Stephen King endorsement angle and the Oprah style "look under your seats" was a little on the pandery side, but it didn't detract much. Might have even added a small tinge to the tension in the atmosphere.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. While I enjoyed the tale, I really wish you made original excerpts from the book (I would give a pass on the final quote, 100% due to the following line). That was disappointing enough to land me in neutral territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mr. King confirmed insane, no?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah. That was just a sweet cherry on top of this Fifthist cupcake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god was this just leading up to a pun?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good IMO. I'd imagine that he also needed a self-help book after world-renowed writer Stephen King was once hit by a car. Just something to consider.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enjoyed this from first to last.

That last line… oh, let me put on my hat so I can take it off for you.

(The idea of a Book what Eats your Mind has intrigued me since discovering Lovecraft as a teenager. The only one I ever found in real life, though, was "Atlas Shrugged". I… eventually got better.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed it as a draft and I like it here. Particularly enjoyed King's voice, I feel like you really nailed his speech pattern and I found it very easy to imagine him saying all of his lines.

item.title: Conwell keeps his copy next to his coffee pot…
item.content:encoded:
Welp, it’s a new year. And that means it’s time for a new tale. This was originally created as Christmas Gift for Silberescher. With his permission, I’ve posted it here, a week or so after the Christmas exchange concluded so that it wouldn’t get lost in the deluge of Xmas Exchange tales. BTW, you should also read those tales too, they are fantastic.

Special thanks to Roget, Randomini, Sirens, TyGently, A Random Day, TwistedGears, Doctor Cimmerian and everyone else who provided feedback when this was a draft.

Enjoy the tale.

JC
